title: Pi child dispatch (runner.ts/dispatch.ts) never writes a gate-events.log audit line, unlike every sibling child-invoking path
evidence: runner.ts:runPiChild() spawns the isolated Pi author/reviewer child, tracks task/model/provider/pid/correlationId/terminal state (completed|degraded) and a per-child DispatchTerminal (accepted/changes_requested/blocked/cancelled/timeout/depth_exceeded/oversized/protocol_error/crashed/degraded) in dispatch.ts, but neither module ever calls appendFile/fs writes anywhere. Grepping runner.ts and dispatch.ts for 'gate-events'/'appendFile'/'log(' returns no matches. Contrast with the two other child-invoking paths in the same package: bridge.ts:213-233 (auditFailure) appends a structured line ([timestamp]|HOST: pi|RULE|AUDIT|CORRELATION|REQUEST_BYTES|STDOUT_BYTES|STDERR_BYTES) to .codearbiter/gate-events.log on every bridge failure, and compaction.ts:436-451 (appendPiCompactionAudit) appends a similar line (AUDIT codes, CORRELATION, PLAN fingerprint, METRICS) to the same sink after every native-compaction cycle, success or fail. runner.ts additionally discards child stderr content entirely -- child.stderr.on('data') (runner.ts:763-766) only accumulates a byte counter for overflow detection and never stores or surfaces any of the bytes, so even the process-tree cleanup reason (protocol_error/timeout/startup_failure/cancelled/protocol_overflow, ProcessTreeCleanupReason) that finishFailure() computes is returned only inline to the calling agent turn via childFailure()'s single fixed diagnostic string ('Pi child isolation failed safely; no inline promotion is available; run /ca-doctor.') and is never persisted anywhere.
impact: An operator investigating what an author/reviewer Pi child actually did -- which role, model, provider, correlationId, terminal state (crashed vs timeout vs protocol_error vs blocked), duration, or why a dispatch degraded -- has no durable record to inspect after the fact; the only evidence is the returned DispatchResult JSON surfaced transiently to the calling Pi agent turn. This is the exact class of gap the project's own audit-trail doctrine (observability-001/#186, security-controls.md 'Audit trail') was built to close for hooks and bridge calls, but the newest and highest-risk child-spawning surface in this branch (per inventory.md risk ranking #1: 'plugins/ca-pi/tools/src/ -- NEW, child-process spawning') was not brought under it. Post-incident reconstruction of dispatch behavior is impossible without re-running with instrumentation.
recommendation: Add a bounded, fail-open audit write to .codearbiter/gate-events.log from runPiChild()/dispatch.ts's runRole(), mirroring bridge.ts's auditFailure()/compaction.ts's appendPiCompactionAudit() shape: correlationId, role, provider/model, terminal state, duration, and a redacted/bounded slice of stderr (already byte-capped at MAX_STDERR_BYTES=16384) on both success and failure paths, wrapped so a locked/missing log never changes dispatch's return value.
acceptance_criteria: runPiChild() or dispatch.ts's runRole() appends one line per child run to .codearbiter/gate-events.log recording at minimum: correlationId, role/launchKind, provider, model, terminal state, and duration
the audit write is best-effort/fail-open (a write failure does not change the returned ChildResult/DispatchChild)
a redacted, bounded excerpt of child stderr is retained in the audit line or a companion diagnostic field rather than discarded outright
test-fidelity coverage exercises the new audit write for at least one success and one failure terminal state
